subject: Anthropology
title: Class and Inequality
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Class and Inequality.

hook.text: Caste is fixed. Class is supposed to be flexible. But in practice, class can be just as sticky as caste.

buildup.text: Class systems rank people by wealth, education, and occupation. In theory, movement between classes is possible.

discovery.text: Marx defined class by your relation to production: owners exploit workers. Weber added status and political power.

twist.text: Meritocracy is the promise, but inheritance, networks, and education reproduce class across generations.

climax.text: Class is the engine of modern inequality. It shapes your health, education, lifespan, and opportunities from birth.

punchline.text: Class moves, but it rarely moves far.

quiz.question: How does class differ from caste?
options: Class is theoretically open to mobility while caste is fixed at birth
